message:
fix(mcp): CloseIssueTool reads "what was blocked by this issue" via get_blocked_issue_ids, not get_dependents

`CloseIssueTool` constructs a `next_actions` hint after closing
an issue by listing the issues that the closed one was blocking
(so the operator knows what was unblocked).  The old call
`storage.get_dependents(id)` returns the wrong direction of the
dependency edge — `get_dependents(id)` is "issues that this
issue depends on", which is exactly the inverse of what
`CloseIssueTool` wants to surface.

`get_blocked_issue_ids(id)` returns "issues that this issue is
blocking" (i.e., the ids whose `blocked_by` includes the closed
id), which is the correct direction.

Net behavioural change: closing issue X now correctly reports
"these issues are now unblocked" for the issues whose blocked_by
contained X, instead of the irrelevant "these are X's
dependencies".  Result format and downstream consumers are
unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
